item.title: Taking a Week to Count Votes Is Doing It Wrong
item.description: It is time for California to catch up to its own past.

item.title: A Different Kind of ‘Values Voter’ Will Power the 2026 Midterms
item.description: A new politics of morality is rising in the Democratic Party.

item.title: The Supreme Court Doesn’t Own the Constitution
item.description: It starts with ‘We the People’ for a reason.

item.title: Wall Street’s A.I. Bet Is About to Become Yours
item.description: David Wallace-Wells speaks with the economist and law professor Natasha Sarin about what the coming A.I. I.P.O.s could mean for your retirement account.

item.title: I Worked for the C.I.A. for 28 Years. Bill Pulte Is a Dangerous Choice.
item.description: President Trump’s decision to place Bill Pulte at the head of the U.S. intelligence community is not just another poor personnel choice. It’s a warning.

item.title: ‘A Mighty Form of Communion’: Three Writers on the World Cup’s Strange Magic
item.description: There’s ample cause for celebration and consternation alike.

item.title: The Global Bull Market That A.I. Obscures
item.description: The rest of the world has been writing one of the more remarkable chapters in modern financial history — and many have hardly noticed.

item.title: Is the U.S.-Israel Alliance Still Strategic?
item.description: Is the U.S.-Israel alliance doing more harm than good? Matt Duss, the executive vice president at the Center for International Policy, joins “The Ezra Klein Show” to discuss whether the strategic and moral costs of the U.S.-Israel alliance outweigh the benefits.

item.title: For America’s 250th, Let’s Get Something Clear About Our National Identity
item.description: It depends on both ideas and cultural inheritance.

item.title: Everybody Is a Loser in This Middle East War
item.description: None of the participants in the recent wars of the Middle East can claim victory. All are to blame.

item.title: The Dangers of Ignoring America’s Past
item.description: Readers, including Drew Faust, a former president of Harvard, respond to a column by David French. Also: President Trump’s tax burden; time to rebuild trust.

item.title: The Left’s Reckoning on Gaza, Elites and American Power
item.description: Matt Duss, Senator Bernie Sanders’s former foreign policy adviser, argues that Democrats need a foreign policy reckoning.

item.title: Trump’s Assaults on Scientific Research Just Got Worse
item.description: Proposed rules would ravage the world’s most productive scientific enterprise.

item.title: If Winning Is Everything, Is Anything Off Limits?
item.description: The “fake news” revolution is in full swing.

item.title: The Good That Can Come From Platner’s Candidacy
item.description: The Senate race in Maine is a chance to do away with inconsistent standards applied according to political bias.

item.title: Bronze Age Pervert’s ‘Cosplay’ Masculinity
item.description: “This is an ideology that is born out of fat modernity itself,” the journalist Helen Lewis argues on “The Ezra Klein Show,” where she explains why the online right’s fascination with hypermasculinity is a bored delusion made possible by the safety of modern life.

item.title: Quit Complaining About the World Cup
item.description: There is a lot to gripe about. There’s also a lot to like.

item.title: We Need A Menopause Revolution
item.description: Why don’t women get the care they deserve during menopause? According to Melinda French Gates, even medical providers don’t receive the training they need — at the expense of women’s health and well-being.

item.title: Meet Europe’s New Machiavelli
item.description: Luuk van Middelaar, a Dutch scholar, is emerging as the continent’s master strategist.

item.title: What Liberals Get Wrong About the Middle Class
item.description: The middle class is larger than you think, and growing.

item.title: Red State Democrats Need a New Approach to Abortion
item.description: Try nuance and compromise.

item.title: What’s the Left’s Vision for Foreign Policy After Trump?
item.description: Matt Duss, Senator Bernie Sanders’s former foreign policy adviser, argues that Democrats need a foreign policy reckoning.

item.title: There’s a Better Way to Catch Seafood
item.description: Bottom trawlers kill or maim everything in their path. We should ban — or at least curtail — the incredibly harmful practice.

item.title: The SpaceX I.P.O. Shows Us What’s Wrong With Our Stock Market
item.description: The SpaceX I.P.O. proves the stock market has become a hype machine.

item.title: The A.I. Classroom Is Quiet. Way Too Quiet.
item.description: A.I. tutoring is seductive, but ultimately ineffective.

item.title: The States Where Life Is Better
item.description: Trust, well-being and mental health are all down in America. But some states are better to live in than others, according to a new study.

item.title: Why People Are Obsessed With Platner
item.description: Graham Platner is not just a candidate for the Senate in Maine, argues the Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie on “The Opinions,” but also a representative of “an intra-Democratic Party factional battle.” Bouie says the arguments around Platner resemble a proxy fight unfolding within the party.

item.title: Trump’s Eruption, and His Sycophants
item.description: Readers discuss President Trump’s explosive interview on NBC and his fawning cabinet. Also: Weakened voting rights; Mr. Trump on a $250 bill.

item.title: Why We May Need More Military Veterans in Congress
item.description: There’s an argument for candidates who’ve served in the military.

item.title: Putin Restricted the Internet. It Has Not Gone to Plan.
item.description: In destroying what remained of a relatively free internet, Vladimir Putin has broken a longstanding social contract.

item.title: Why Everyone Wants Jon Ossoff to Run for President
item.description: The Georgia senator is excoriating Trump and his systemic corruption in a way that transcends the Democratic Party’s progressive-moderate divide.

item.title: An Aggressive Israel Is Shaking the Middle East
item.description: Since Hamas attacked Israel on Oct. 7, 2023, aggression and expansionism have come to define Israel’s foreign policy.

item.title: For Trump, the World Is for the Taking
item.description: Trump has ushered in a new era of fuzzy foreign policy. In an era when powerful leaders are tempted to blur the lines of legal borders, the political scientists Stephen E. Hanson and Jeffrey S. Kopstein warn about the dangerous ramifications of their taking what they please.

item.title: The Postpartum Care That Mothers Need
item.description: Readers respond to an doctor’s essay about the health care system’s failures to meet the needs of postpartum mothers. Also: Hungary as a role model.

item.title: My Life Lessons From M.M.A. Fighting
item.description: There are lessons in competitive fighting that I never expected to learn.

item.title: The Tony Awards Nominees Are Not Dangerous This Year
item.description: The Tony Awards nominees are not dangerous this year.

item.title: You Can Walk to the World Cup in New Jersey. But Should You?
item.description: We decided to make the trek and find out.

item.title: Erdogan and Putin, the End of an Unlikely Partnership
item.description: Russia’s decline has given Turkey the freedom to pursue its interests, and Ukraine is the beneficiary.

item.title: Who Will Account for Another Foreign Policy Failure?
item.description: The American foreign policy class should shed its addiction to military force.

item.title: A Dark Vision of Masculinity
item.description: Why does the modern, online vision of masculinity feel so “deeply depressing”? In conversation with the journalist Helen Lewis, the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein explains how leading right-wing figures weaponize the word “virtue” to mask a lack of moral and civic obligation.

item.title: Behind Every Dad Bod Is a Healthy Dad Brain
item.description: The benefits of fatherhood are deep and long lasting.

item.title: Graham Platner and the Rise of the ‘Dirtbag’ Democrat
item.description: And what the Maine candidate reveals about politics today.

item.title: Escape From Trumpism
item.description: The best pathway out is to make Congress great again.

item.title: Norma Jeane’s Still Got It!
item.description: Some continue to like it hot.

item.title: When Is It Wrong to Use A.I.?
item.description: How to criticize an inevitable disruption.

item.title: Who Should Get an A at Harvard?
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay about the university’s new 20 percent cap on A grades.

item.title: Graham Platner and the Rise of the ‘Dirtbag’ Democrat
item.description: And what the Maine candidate reveals about politics today.

item.title: Trump Has Become What He Feared
item.description: “Trump loses more often, on more things, than most.”

item.title: A Divide Over U.S. Support for Israel
item.description: Readers respond to Senator Chris Van Hollen’s call on the Democratic Party to push Israel to work on a two-state solution. Also: Options for mothers.
